message:
fuzz: add crypto_hpke_open and crypto_shamir_reconstruct harnesses

Two new targets covering highest-value unfuzzed attacker-controlled
surfaces in fcp-crypto:

* fuzz_crypto_hpke_open drives HpkeSealedBox::from_bytes on adversarial
  wire bytes, verifies to_bytes is idempotent across parse round-trips,
  and calls hpke_open against a deterministic recipient key. A successful
  decrypt on random bytes would indicate Poly1305 tag forgery; the
  harness asserts against it.

* fuzz_crypto_shamir_reconstruct drives ShamirShare::from_bytes and
  reconstruct_secret on attacker-chosen combinations (duplicate indices,
  mismatched lengths, reserved index 0, oversized share sets). Also
  runs a positive split then reconstruct round-trip property and a
  soundness check that duplicate-index subsets are rejected rather than
  interpolated into a forged secret.

Both compile clean under the fuzz workspace's nightly toolchain.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -218,5 +218,19 @@ test = false
 doc = false
 bench = false
 
+[[bin]]
+name = "fuzz_crypto_hpke_open"
+path = "fuzz_targets/crypto_hpke_open.rs"
+test = false
+doc = false
+bench = false
+
+[[bin]]
+name = "fuzz_crypto_shamir_reconstruct"
+path = "fuzz_targets/crypto_shamir_reconstruct.rs"
+test = false
+doc = false
+bench = false
+
 [workspace]
 members = ["."]
